FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Release 2.0, children aged 9-11 years (mean 9.9 SD 0.6), n=4262 with genetically assigned European ancestry

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: cohort study using Gsens genetic sensitivity analysis model incorporating polygenic risk scores for both exposure and outcomes

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed publication in JAMA Network Open, November 2023

FT_IC2.evidence: Exposure is generic "child-reported daily screen time (in hours)" from questionnaires at baseline, not isolated to smartphone or social media modality. Criteria require phone or SM-specific operationalization; composite generic screen time without per-modality breakdown fails FT-IC2.

notes: Although outcomes (attention problems, internalizing problems measured by ACHENBACH at 1-year follow-up) qualify as individual-trait-level psychiatric measures, the exposure fails FT-IC2 because screen time is not operationalized as phone or SM-specific activity.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study Release 3.0, participants aged 9-10 at baseline with structural imaging at T0 and T2, n=5,166 after QC; longitudinal design

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: Joint and Individual Variation Explained (JIVE) to identify brain co-development patterns, generalized linear mixed-effect models with mediation analysis

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed publication in Journal of Behavioral Addictions, 2023

FT_IC2.evidence: Baseline SMA (screen media activity) is IV; measured as total non-school-related screen media time on typical weekday/weekend, including TV, movies, videos online, video games, texting, social media, video chatting. DV is internalizing psychopathology (measured by parent-reported CBCL at two-year follow-up), which is individual-trait-level.

FT_IC3.evidence: Quantitative results reported: baseline SMA associated with internalizing psychopathology at year 2 (β=0.020, SE=0.008, p=0.014); SMA related to structural co-development pattern (β=0.015, SE=0.007, p=0.029); indirect effect = 0.020, p-value = 0.043

FT_IC4.evidence: Numeric quantitative results for SM↔outcome relationship: coefficient β, standard error, p-values, and indirect/mediation effects reported with statistical significance

FT_EC7_reason: Longitudinal design with baseline SMA as IV predicting future internalizing outcome; SMA is not DV-only, so EC7 exclusion does not apply.
notes: Paper examines mediation pathway via brain co-development pattern; relatively small effect sizes but meets all substantive criteria.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study baseline data collection, multi-site (21 sites across US), children aged 9-10 years (n=11,780), Release 2.0

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: cross-sectional associations between screen time activities and K-SADS-5 clinical diagnoses using univariate general linear models with demographic covariates; exposure-response curves

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed publication in Journal of Affective Disorders, 2022

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time measured separately for specific modalities: TV/movies, watching videos online, video games, texting, social media, video chatting. Paper reports both total screen time and digital media composites (social media, gaming, texting, online videos). Each pairs with K-SADS-5 diagnoses (depressive disorders, anxiety, suicidal ideation/attempts, self-harm)—all individual-trait-level psychiatric outcomes measured via semi-structured clinical interview.

FT_IC3.evidence: Quantitative results reported throughout: adjusted relative risk (ARR) with 95% CI for all screen time categories and specific digital media activities. Examples: girls ≥2h screen media ARR=2.00 (1.48–2.70) for depressive disorders; digital media use ARR=1.96 (1.53–2.51) for depressive in girls; social media use ARR=2.20 (1.13–4.30) in girls

FT_IC4.evidence: Numeric quantitative results for SM/screen↔outcome relationships: RR, ARR values, confidence intervals, p-values, exposure-response curves comparing hours/day categories (0 to 7+ hours)

FT_EC7_reason: Cross-sectional baseline study design; FT-EC7 applies only to longitudinal designs where SM is DV-only with no SM-as-IV analysis anywhere.
notes: Comprehensive cross-sectional analysis with stratification by gender; multiple digital media types examined separately and in composite; strong associations with psychiatric diagnoses measured by gold-standard clinical interview.
